name: Chicken Medaillons
recipeInstructions:
1. Pound 4 chicken breast halves (12 oz total) with a meat mallet to an even thickness of about 1/8 inch.
2. For the filling, in a medium saucepan, cook mushrooms, carrot, onion, and celery in a small amount of boiling water for about 5 minutes or until tender. Drain well.
3. Stir in 1 1/2 tablespoons lemon juice, half of the thyme, and 1/8 teaspoon pepper into the vegetables.
4. Spoon some filling onto each chicken piece, fold in the sides, and roll up securely with wooden toothpicks.
5. Spray a medium skillet with nonstick coating and brown the chicken over medium heat for 3-4 minutes, turning occasionally.
6. Add the remaining lemon juice, thyme, bouillon granules, and 1/2 cup water to the skillet. Cover and simmer about 15 minutes or until the chicken is no longer pink.
7. Remove the toothpicks from the chicken.
8. Combine cornstarch and milk in a small bowl. Stir into the skillet until bubbly and cooked for an additional 2 minutes.
9. Cut the chicken into 1/2-inch slices and arrange on top of the sauce.
Per serving: 174 calories, 28 grams protein, 6 grams carbohydrates, 3 grams fat, 73 milligrams cholesterol, 192 milligrams sodium, 426 milligrams potassium.
